message.content:
Adversaries may employ the technique of encrypting data on target systems to disrupt availability and extort victims, commonly known as ransomware attacks. This involves encrypting files on local and remote drives, often targeting user files like Office documents, PDFs, images, and source code. The attackers then demand a ransom for the decryption key. In severe cases, critical system files, disk partitions, and the Master Boot Record (MBR) may also be encrypted. To maximize impact, ransomware can propagate across networks using techniques like Valid Accounts, OS Credential Dumping, and SMB/Windows Admin Shares. Adversaries may also use internal defacement tactics, such as changing wallpapers or sending ransom notes to printers.

Mitigation strategies include regular data backups, network segmentation, robust access controls, and user training to recognize phishing attempts. Employing endpoint detection and response (EDR) tools, maintaining up-to-date antivirus software, and implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA) can also help prevent and mitigate the impact of such attacks.
